steps.title: Identify the target population vs. the sample
steps.content: The council wanted to assess opinions of all town residents, but only surveyed residents who own skateboards. These are not the same group. All residents who do not own skateboards were excluded from the sample.

steps.title: Identify why this creates bias
steps.content: Skateboard owners have a direct personal interest in a skate park. Their preferences systematically differ from those of residents who do not own skateboards. A sample drawn entirely from one interested subgroup cannot represent the full population.

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• A: We cannot conclude the majority of all residents favor the skate park based on a biased sample. The sample does not represent all residents. Eliminated.
  • B: More skateboard owners would not fix the bias — it would make it worse by further over-representing one group. Eliminated.
  • C: Surveying only residents who do not own skateboards would create the opposite bias. The goal is a representative sample, not one skewed the other way. Eliminated.
  • D: The sample is biased because it draws exclusively from skateboard owners, who are not representative of all town residents. That confirms D is correct.
